hypothesis: Prescriptive lessons without enforcement mechanisms decay faster in citation count than enforced prescriptive lessons, confirming L-601 (voluntary protocol decay) at the lesson level
method: Classified 1282 lessons into prescriptive-enforced (>=2 prescriptive + >=2 enforcement patterns), prescriptive-unenforced, and descriptive. Built citation graph. Measured mean incoming citations and zero-cite rates by category and era.
prediction: Unenforced prescriptions have >30% lower mean citation than enforced; dead-letter count >20

mid_S301_S450.note: Unenforced prescriptions get initial attention then decay — mid-era highest, recent-era lowest

effect_size: 3.8x citation ratio in recent era (enforced/unenforced), +19.7% enforcement lift on zero-cite avoidance
surprise: Mid-era unenforced prescriptions had HIGHEST mean citations (5.10) — initial attention burst then decay. Enforced prescriptions sustain. This is L-601 in action: voluntary prescriptions get read once then forgotten.
